Class LibKstatFunctions

java.lang.Object
oshi.ffm.ForeignFunctions
oshi.ffm.util.platform.unix.solaris.LibKstatFunctions
public final class LibKstatFunctions extends ForeignFunctions
FFM bindings for Solaris/illumos libkstat, the kernel-statistics library.

Mirrors the surface of com.sun.jna.platform.unix.solaris.LibKstat so KstatUtilFFM can offer the same API as the JNA KstatUtil. Chain operations are not thread-safe; KstatUtilFFM synchronizes externally.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class ForeignFunctions

    ForeignFunctions.ArenaBooleanCallable, ForeignFunctions.ArenaCallable<T>, ForeignFunctions.ArenaDoubleCallable, ForeignFunctions.ArenaIntCallable, ForeignFunctions.ArenaLongCallable, ForeignFunctions.ArenaRunnable

    Layout of kstat_io_t (<kstat.h>). 80 bytes total on LP64 — no internal padding because the long and int fields naturally align.

      • Constant Field Values

    • KSTAT_LAYOUT

      public static final StructLayout KSTAT_LAYOUT
      Layout of struct kstat (the kstat header). All field offsets follow the C struct definition in <kstat.h> on LP64 Solaris/illumos. Total size: 184 bytes.

    • KSTAT_NAMED_LAYOUT

      public static final StructLayout KSTAT_NAMED_LAYOUT
      Layout of struct kstat_named. Total size: 48 bytes. The value union occupies 16 bytes starting at offset 32; readers must select the correct accessor based on namedDataType(MemorySegment).

    • KSTAT_NAMED_SIZE

      public static final long KSTAT_NAMED_SIZE
      Byte size of a kstat_named record (48).

    • KSTAT_IO_LAYOUT

      public static final StructLayout KSTAT_IO_LAYOUT
      Layout of kstat_io_t (<kstat.h>). 80 bytes total on LP64 — no internal padding because the long and int fields naturally align.

  • Method Details

    • kstatSnaptime

      public static long kstatSnaptime(MemorySegment ksp)
      Reads ks_snaptime from a kstat header.
      Parameters:
      ksp - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the snapshot time, in nanoseconds since boot

    • kstatIoNread

      public static long kstatIoNread(MemorySegment io)
      Reads nread from a kstat_io_t segment.
      Parameters:
      io - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_IO_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the number of bytes read

    • kstatIoNwritten

      public static long kstatIoNwritten(MemorySegment io)
      Reads nwritten from a kstat_io_t segment.
      Parameters:
      io - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_IO_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the number of bytes written

    • kstatIoReads

      public static int kstatIoReads(MemorySegment io)
      Reads reads from a kstat_io_t segment.
      Parameters:
      io - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_IO_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the number of read operations

    • kstatIoWrites

      public static int kstatIoWrites(MemorySegment io)
      Reads writes from a kstat_io_t segment.
      Parameters:
      io - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_IO_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the number of write operations

    • kstatIoRtime

      public static long kstatIoRtime(MemorySegment io)
      Reads rtime from a kstat_io_t segment.
      Parameters:
      io - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_IO_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      cumulative run (service) time, in nanoseconds

    • kstatIoWcnt

      public static int kstatIoWcnt(MemorySegment io)
      Reads wcnt from a kstat_io_t segment.
      Parameters:
      io - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_IO_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the count of elements in the wait queue

    • kstatIoRcnt

      public static int kstatIoRcnt(MemorySegment io)
      Reads rcnt from a kstat_io_t segment.
      Parameters:
      io - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_IO_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the count of elements in the run queue

    • kstat_open

      public static MemorySegment kstat_open() throws Throwable
      Opens a kstat control structure.
      Returns:
      a MemorySegment addressing the kstat_ctl_t, or MemorySegment.NULL on failure
      Throws:
      Throwable - on FFM invocation error

    • kstat_close

      public static int kstat_close(MemorySegment kc) throws Throwable
      Frees all resources associated with the given kstat control structure.
      Parameters:
      kc - the kstat control structure
      Returns:
      0 on success, -1 on failure
      Throws:
      Throwable - on FFM invocation error

    • kstat_chain_update

      public static int kstat_chain_update(MemorySegment kc) throws Throwable
      Syncs the user kstat header chain with the kernel's.
      Parameters:
      kc - the kstat control structure
      Returns:
      the new KCID if the chain changed, 0 if unchanged, or -1 on failure
      Throws:
      Throwable - on FFM invocation error

    • kstat_lookup

      public static MemorySegment kstat_lookup(MemorySegment kc, MemorySegment module, int instance, MemorySegment name) throws Throwable
      Searches the chain for a kstat matching (module, instance, name).
      Parameters:
      kc - the kstat control structure
      module - the module name, or MemorySegment.NULL to wildcard
      instance - the instance number, or -1 to wildcard
      name - the kstat name, or MemorySegment.NULL to wildcard
      Returns:
      a pointer to the matching kstat, or MemorySegment.NULL if none
      Throws:
      Throwable - on FFM invocation error

    • kstat_read

      public static int kstat_read(MemorySegment kc, MemorySegment ksp, MemorySegment buf) throws Throwable
      Reads data for the kstat pointed to by ksp from the kernel.
      Parameters:
      kc - the kstat control structure
      ksp - the kstat header
      buf - if non-NULL, data is copied here; otherwise allocated in ksp.ks_data
      Returns:
      the current KCID on success, -1 on failure
      Throws:
      Throwable - on FFM invocation error

    • kstat_data_lookup

      public static MemorySegment kstat_data_lookup(MemorySegment ksp, MemorySegment name) throws Throwable
      Searches a named-data kstat for the record with the given key.
      Parameters:
      ksp - the kstat to search
      name - the key
      Returns:
      a pointer to the record (a kstat_named_t for KSTAT_TYPE_NAMED), or MemorySegment.NULL if not found
      Throws:
      Throwable - on FFM invocation error

    • kstatNext

      public static MemorySegment kstatNext(MemorySegment ksp)
      Returns the ks_next pointer of the kstat header, reinterpreted to KSTAT_LAYOUT.
      Parameters:
      ksp - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      next kstat segment (already reinterpreted), or MemorySegment.NULL if end of chain

    • kstatInstance

      public static int kstatInstance(MemorySegment ksp)
      Returns ks_instance from a kstat header.
      Parameters:
      ksp - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the module instance number

    • kstatType

      public static byte kstatType(MemorySegment ksp)
      Returns ks_type from a kstat header.
      Parameters:
      ksp - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the kstat data type (e.g. KSTAT_TYPE_NAMED)

    • kstatData

      public static MemorySegment kstatData(MemorySegment ksp)
      Returns the ks_data pointer of the kstat header.
      Parameters:
      ksp - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the data segment as a raw pointer (size unknown until reinterpreted)

    • kstatNdata

      public static int kstatNdata(MemorySegment ksp)
      Returns ks_ndata from a kstat header.
      Parameters:
      ksp - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the number of data records

    • kstatModule

      public static String kstatModule(MemorySegment ksp)
      Reads ks_module as a NUL-terminated string.
      Parameters:
      ksp - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the module name

    • kstatName

      public static String kstatName(MemorySegment ksp)
      Reads ks_name as a NUL-terminated string.
      Parameters:
      ksp - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the kstat name

    • kstatClass

      public static String kstatClass(MemorySegment ksp)
      Reads ks_class as a NUL-terminated string.
      Parameters:
      ksp - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the kstat class

    • namedDataType

      public static byte namedDataType(MemorySegment named)
      Returns the data_type tag of a kstat_named_t record.
      Parameters:
      named - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_NAMED_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the data-type tag (one of the KSTAT_DATA_* constants)

    • namedName

      public static String namedName(MemorySegment named)
      Reads the name field of a kstat_named_t record as a NUL-terminated string.
      Parameters:
      named - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_NAMED_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the record name

    • namedValueInt32

      public static int namedValueInt32(MemorySegment named)
      Reads the union of a kstat_named_t record as int32.
      Parameters:
      named - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_NAMED_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the value as a 32-bit signed integer

    • namedValueInt64

      public static long namedValueInt64(MemorySegment named)
      Reads the union of a kstat_named_t record as int64.
      Parameters:
      named - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_NAMED_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the value as a 64-bit signed integer

    • namedValueChar

      public static String namedValueChar(MemorySegment named)
      Reads the union as a KSTAT_DATA_CHAR buffer (16 bytes, NUL-terminated).
      Parameters:
      named - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_NAMED_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the string

    • namedValueString

      public static String namedValueString(MemorySegment named)
      Reads the union as a KSTAT_DATA_STRING (struct kstat_named.value.str): pointer + length. The pointer addresses a string elsewhere in the kstat data buffer.
      Parameters:
      named - segment reinterpreted to KSTAT_NAMED_LAYOUT
      Returns:
      the string, or empty if the pointer is NULL

    • isLibKstatLoaded

      public static boolean isLibKstatLoaded()
      Returns true if libkstat.so.1 loaded successfully. Effectively always true on Solaris and illumos systems where this module is exercised; included for symmetry with Kstat2Functions.HAS_KSTAT2.
      Returns:
      whether libkstat.so.1 was loadable at class-init time
